Dan's decision placemat
Underwriting Operating Catalog Rankings
Services × Multi-unit

A drawing board for the business path.

Two arenas, not one blended list.

Arena 1

Services

Recurring local service businesses: route density, customer books, skilled field labor, compliance/maintenance, home and property systems.

Default posture: buy first, franchise second, DIY only selectively.

RoutesTechniciansReviewsCustomer files
Arena 2

Multi-unit

Repeatable consumer-facing units: food, retail, education, wellness, specialty trade retail, membership concepts.

Default posture: DIY first where testable, franchise where system value is real, acquisition/resale selectively.

BrandSite selectionUnit economicsPlaybook creation

The 2×3 decision matrix.

DIY / build
Franchise
Acquisition / buy
Services
Selective only

Works for simple, pre-sellable routes: mosquito, soft wash, holiday lighting, maybe irrigation if a field lead exists.

Must prove: demand before labor.

Can work

Use when SOPs, territory, training, insurance/compliance, or lead gen are worth royalties.

Risk: paying for a logo.

Best fit

Buy customer books, techs, licenses, routes, reviews, and cash flow. Then modernize.

Risk: owner-dependent job.

Multi-unit
Best fit

Best when brand, product, content, community, and first-unit playbook are the upside.

Examples: candy, kids STEM, sports academy.

Selective

Use only if training, sourcing, curriculum, site selection, or benchmarking changes the odds.

Example: Metal Supermarkets.

Selective

Buy resales when reviews, lease/site, staff, or provider base matter more than concept creation.

Example: medspa.

Current strategic take.

Current priority wedge: property-manager compliance routes + affluent-home systems: Backflow, fire/life-safety, hood cleaning, grease trap, stormwater, and AED compliance are tracked manually by many property managers. The software/calendar layer can become the acquisition channel for the service work.

Services / acquisition

Priority vein: property-manager compliance routes — fire/life-safety, backflow, kitchen hood, grease trap, stormwater — plus drainage/irrigation as affluent-home exterior systems.

Multi-unit / DIY

The universe covers 179 categories across services and multi-unit arenas, scored on 12 attractiveness dimensions plus 3 path-fit scores.

Franchise

Middle path only when the operating system is worth the drag. Hood cleaning and Metal Supermarkets deserve FDD checks; generic seasonal service franchises need skepticism.

Highest-ranked opportunities.

This is not a next-steps bucket. It is the top of the full ranked universe using UW Score (economic underwriting quality) first, then Vibe Score (strategic attractiveness) as tiebreaker. Path fit shows execution feasibility for the selected route. Use the master catalog below for full detail.

Full category universe.

Full ranked list across Services and Multi-unit. Default sort: UW Score (economic underwriting quality) → Vibe Score (strategic attractiveness / interestingness). Path fit = how feasible the best execution route is. Filter by arena/path/market/search; open any row for economic detail, proof points, and kill tests.
